page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7t.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance consistently in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance scores are 1890.78 and 7769.13 respectively, while the Geekbench multi-core score reaches 5655.0. However, a notable performance tradeoff exists in memory bandwidth; while small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth is average at 423.1 GB/sec, large-block uncached read-only bandwidth drops to a weak 33.45 GB/sec. Additionally, LLM inference speeds for medium (7B) models are poor to weak, with prompt processing at 16.12 tokens/sec and text generation at 6.54 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density make this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The average performance in database operations (3875.6 on PassMark) and Redis SET operations (7.96 million operations per second) indicates suitability for in-memory caching, database hosting, and general-purpose enterprise applications. It is less suited for large-scale language model inference or workloads heavily reliant on high-throughput uncached memory reads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7t.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this server provides 8.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU resources. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across standard compute, database operations, and Redis caching. However, testing reveals a performance tradeoff with weak large-block uncached memory read bandwidth of 33.45 GB/sec and poor medium-model LLM inference speeds. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, and web serving workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU resources and high memory density without requiring local storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7t.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for database and in-memory caching workloads.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 virtual server featuring high memory density and balanced compute performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs and 64 GB RAM
High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Average performance in Redis and database operations
Weak performance in large-block uncached memory reads
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and web serving
